skill.name: sanger-chromatogram-qa
skill.description: Automated quality assessment for Sanger sequencing chromatograms with mixed-signal detection, base-call validation, and variant identification against reference sequences.
skill.summary: Quality scoring and mutation detection for Sanger sequencing data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 39 static analysis findings are false positives after manual evaluation. The skill is a legitimate bioinformatics tool for Sanger sequencing quality assessment. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential access, or malicious intent detected. The Python script (scripts/main.py) performs only local numpy computation with no network calls. Skill documentation includes a GitHub source URL and references standard Python execution commands within markdown code blocks.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm in documentation text
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 4 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' on lines containing argparse, skill metadata, and evaluation notes. None of the flagged lines contain any cryptographic algorithm implementation.

low_findings.title: False positive: Ruby backtick execution in markdown code blocks
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 24 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' on SKILL.md. All flagged lines are markdown fenced code blocks (triple backticks) containing standard Python validation commands (py_compile, --help, pip install). These are not Ruby backtick operators.

low_findings.title: False positive: Path traversal sequence in security checklist
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged '../' on SKILL.md lines 114 and 117. These are within a security checklist that documents items to guard against ('No unauthorized file system access (../)', 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)'). The text describes defensive measures, not actual path traversal.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance in metadata and documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 4 instances of 'System reconnaissance' on lines in the audit result JSON file and SKILL.md containing standard evaluation language and documentation text. No reconnaissance commands or patterns are present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Hardcoded URL in skill documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 2 hardcoded URL instances on SKILL.md line 7. The URL points to the skill's GitHub source repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a legitimate source attribution, not a malicious network endpoint.

content.user_title: Analyze Sanger sequencing quality and detect mutations
content.value_statement: Manual inspection of Sanger chromatogram data is slow and inconsistent. This skill automates quality assessment of sequencing traces, detects low-quality bases, and identifies potential mutations against a reference sequence.
actual_capabilities: Assess chromatogram quality scores for Sanger sequencing data
Detect low-quality base calls below quality threshold (Q20)
Identify single nucleotide variants (SNVs) against a reference sequence
Generate structured quality reports with quantitative metrics
Flag mixed-signal regions that may indicate heterozygous variants
Calculate percentage of low-quality bases for sample rejection decisions
limitations: Requires pre-processed quality score arrays, not raw AB1 trace file parsing
Demo mode uses synthetic data instead of real sequencing files
Limited to pairwise sequence comparison with simple SNV calling
Does not support complex structural variant detection or de novo assembly

use_cases.title: Mutation Verification
use_cases.description: Compare Sanger sequencing results against a reference to verify suspected mutations and confirm variant calls.
use_cases.target_user: Research scientist validating suspected mutations from Sanger sequencing data

use_cases.title: Clone Confirmation
use_cases.description: Check that cloned sequences match the expected reference and identify any unwanted base changes.
use_cases.target_user: Lab technician confirming sequence identity of cloned constructs

use_cases.title: Genotyping Quality Control
use_cases.description: Run quality checks on Sanger sequencing results to identify low-quality samples that need re-sequencing.
use_cases.target_user: Core facility manager running quality checks on Sanger sequencing results

prompt_templates.title: Basic quality assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a quality assessment on this Sanger chromatogram data. Quality scores: [paste array of integers]. Check the average quality, count bases below Q20, and calculate the percentage of low-quality bases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick evaluation of sequencing run quality with minimal parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted mutation analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this Sanger sequencing result against the reference sequence to find mutations. Sequence: [paste sequence string]. Reference: [paste reference string]. Report all SNV differences and their positions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs to confirm a suspected single nucleotide variant at a specific locus.

prompt_templates.title: Batch quality comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Run quality assessment on multiple Sanger samples. For each sample, provide the average quality score, number of low-quality bases (below Q20), total base count, and the percentage of low-quality bases. Flag any sample with more than 10 percent low-quality bases for re-sequencing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Core facility processing multiple sequencing samples that need pass-fail quality decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Custom QC with heterozygous detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full quality check and mutation analysis on this Sanger sequencing data. Quality scores: [paste array of integers]. Sequence: [paste sequence string]. Reference: [paste reference string]. Provide the quality report, mutation list with position and type, and identify any positions with mixed-signal patterns that suggest heterozygous variants.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user running comprehensive QC with heterozygous variant screening.

output_examples.output:
Average quality: 30.0
Low quality bases: 2 (16.7%)
Quality assessment: Two bases below Q20 threshold at positions 8 and 9. Sample quality is acceptable but low-quality positions should be reviewed.

output_examples.input:
Sequence: ATCGATCGATCG
Reference: ATCGATCAATCG
Variant position: 234
output_examples.output:
Mutation detected at position 7: Reference A changed to T (SNV).
No mixed-signal regions detected.
Chromatogram quality is adequate for confident base calling at the variant position.
Recommendation: Repeat sequencing to confirm heterozygous call.

best_practices: Validate input quality scores and sequence strings for correct format before analysis.
Confirm reference sequence orientation matches the sequencing read to avoid false mutation calls.
Review flagged low-quality positions manually to confirm base calls at variant sites.
anti_patterns: Running comparison without confirming sequence orientation relative to reference.
Ignoring low-quality scores when calling mutations at those positions.
Using default quality thresholds across different sample types without adjustment.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill works with arrays of quality scores (integers) and plain-text DNA sequences. It does not parse raw AB1 binary trace files directly.

faq.question: What does a quality score below 20 mean?
faq.answer: A quality score below 20 means the base call has less than 99 percent accuracy. These positions have higher error probability and should be reviewed manually.

faq.question: How are heterozygous variants detected?
faq.answer: The skill uses the detect_mixed_signals method to identify positions with overlapping peaks. This indicates potential heterozygous variants that need further investigation.

faq.question: Can this skill work with batch data?
faq.answer: The current script processes one sample at a time. For batch processing, run the quality check on each sample individually and collect the results.

faq.question: What is the difference between SNV and indel?
faq.answer: SNV (single nucleotide variant) is a change at one base position. Indel is an insertion or deletion that changes the sequence length. The skill reports SNV when reference and sequence lengths match.

faq.question: How should I interpret the percent low quality metric?
faq.answer: Percent low quality is the ratio of bases below Q20 to total bases. A high percentage indicates poor sequencing that may need to be repeated for reliable results.
